description: A famous Chinese duck preparation known for its crisp skin and carved slices, traditionally served with thin pancakes, scallions, cucumber, and sweet bean or hoisin-style sauce.
core_ingredients: Seasoning or glaze for the skin
Sweet bean sauce or hoisin-style sauce
preparation_steps: Season and prepare the duck, often drying the skin to encourage crispness.
Roast until the skin is crisp and the meat is cooked through.
Carve the duck into serving pieces or slices.
Serve with pancakes, vegetables, and sauce for wrapping.

nutrition.carbohydrates: Low to moderate; higher when served with pancakes and sauce

health_advice.benefits: Provides protein and is often eaten with fresh vegetables in small wraps.
health_advice.risks: Can be high in fat and sodium, particularly if a lot of skin and sauce are consumed.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Enjoy smaller portions, include more cucumber and scallions, and limit extra sauce.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional serving pancakes and sauces often contain gluten.
